metadata.match_reason: LLM Gateway + Agentic Deployment platform;  Sequoia-backed; serves Resmed, Siemens Healthineers, Nvidia, Zscaler; processes 10B+ requests/mo;  Python/FastAPI/Kubernetes engineers.
metadata.notes: Posted ML Engineer roles ₹35-55L.  LLMOps/RAG playground stack direct match. Co-founders ex-Meta AI infra.

research_summary: TrueFoundry is Series A-backed ($19M, Peak XV + Intel Capital) Bengaluru-based AI infra company building enterprise agentic AI platform: AI Gateway, AI Deploy, MCP Gateway. 40+ employees; team tripled in 2025. Recognized in Gartner AI Gateway Market Guide (Feb 2026). Clients: NVIDIA, Siemens, ResMed, Zscaler. JD wants 4-10yr engineers with production LangGraph/LangChain, RAG, vector DB, and observability experience. Amit's 2-stage Milvus RAG + LangGraph agentic upgrade at Vivansh is a direct match. Email pattern: firstname@truefoundry.com (confirmed via parth@truefoundry.com). 7 contacts found: 3 Tier 1, 3 Tier 3, 1 Tier 4.
jd_full_text:
Senior AI/ML Engineer: LLM & Agent Stack at TrueFoundry (Bengaluru, India)

About TrueFoundry:
TrueFoundry builds Enterprise-Ready Agentic AI infrastructure -- AI Gateway, AI Deploy, MCP Gateway, and Skills Registry. Processes 10B+ requests/month for clients including ResMed, Siemens Healthineers, NVIDIA, and Zscaler. Series A ($19M) backed by Sequoia/Peak XV and Intel Capital.

Role Summary:
Design and own core components that enable enterprise customers to run production agentic AI safely and efficiently, including building robust orchestration for multi-step agents (graph/stateful workflows), model/routing logic, observability and policy enforcement (cost, data residency, rate limiting), and integrating upstream tooling like LangGraph, LangChain, vector stores, and specialized LLM runtimes.

Key Responsibilities:
- Architect and implement scalable agent orchestration patterns (graph-based executors, state management, multi-agent coordination) for production workloads
- Own critical integrations: model adapters, LLM gateway hooks, vector DBs, tools & external APIs, and the platform's LLMops flows
- Build and improve tracing, benchmarking and observability for LLMs and agents, token/cost accounting, latency p95, throughput, and correctness checks
- Drive design for guardrails including moderation hooks, human-in-the-loop checkpoints, and audit trails
- Mentor junior engineers, conduct design reviews, and enhance engineering practices
- Work directly with strategic customers prototyping complex agentic solutions

Must-Have Requirements:
- 4-10 years of software engineering with distributed systems, infrastructure, or ML platform experience
- Deep practical experience deploying LLMs in production (RAG, retrieval, embeddings)
- Hands-on experience with agent orchestration frameworks (LangGraph / LangChain or custom agent runtimes)
- Proven track record building observability, cost controls, and policy enforcement

Preferred Qualifications:
- Open-source contributions to LLM orchestration tools
- Enterprise deployment experience (on-prem/cloud hybrid, data residency, compliance)
- Security, privacy, or model governance background
- Cross-functional project leadership and customer engagement

Location: Bengaluru, India
Salary: 35-55 LPA (from job board listings)
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to lead with LLM and agent stack infrastructure -- JD is about AI Gateway, AI Deploy, MCP Gateway, and Skills Registry at 10B+ requests/month. Skills reordered: LLMs and Techniques first (Agentic AI, LLM Orchestration, multi-model per JD), Languages 2nd, Vector DBs 3rd, Infrastructure 4th; Hardware/IoT dropped. IIT Delhi trimmed to 1 bullet. Projects: WeepScope replaced with SAP Analytics Bot.

contacts.research_hook: Posted ICLR 2025 LLM paper analysis on LinkedIn; builds AutoDeploy and truefailover (AI resilience layer routing around model/region failures in real time) at TrueFoundry; 5 years NLU and MLOps at Haptik before joining. His focus on MLOps tooling at the intersection of distributed systems and ML directly maps to Amit's LangGraph + Milvus orchestration work at Vivansh.
contacts.subject: production LangGraph + Milvus -- relevant to TrueFoundry?
contacts.content:
Hi Chirag,

Saw your ICLR 2025 LLM paper roundup on LinkedIn a few months back. The bit about attention and retrieval efficiency lined up with a problem I was grinding through at the same time -- we had an agentic chatbot on LangGraph hitting 8-15k tokens per request and I needed to know which retrieval patterns were actually worth the cost at that token budget.

I'm 2 years into production AI at Vivansh Infotech (before that, Silver Touch). The system I spent the most time on: a LangGraph state machine orchestrating 2-stage Milvus retrieval with 250+ live permission checks per query, serving 500+ concurrent users. Before that at Silver Touch, I led the migration from in-code Llama-2-7b to a VLLM-served API and benchmarked Milvus vs Qdrant vs FAISS on 50k+ query/month production data -- Milvus won at 95% MRR vs FAISS's 45%.

If TrueFoundry is hiring on the ML platform side this quarter, I'd be glad to hear how you think about the retrieval + agent orchestration problems you're working through. Open to a 15-min call next Tuesday or Thursday.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: production LangGraph + Milvus -- relevant to TrueFoundry?
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Chirag,

Bumping this up in case it got buried. Since I sent the last note, I shipped a small benchmark comparing BGE-M3 vs OpenAI text-embedding-3-large on a domain-specific corpus -- BGE-M3 held up well at a fraction of the cost, which is the kind of tradeoff I imagine comes up a lot when you're designing TrueFoundry's retrieval integrations.

If now's not the right time, no problem.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: production LangGraph + Milvus -- relevant to TrueFoundry?
contacts.follow_up_2_content:

Last note from me. If TrueFoundry isn't actively building out the ML platform team right now, totally fine -- would still appreciate being on your radar.

Good luck with the MCP Gateway work.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Co-launched The Gen Academy in 2025 with Arvind Narayanamurthy -- trained ~2,000 professionals in RAG architectures, agentic systems, MCP, context engineering, and LangChain/LangGraph. Also published an IEEE 2025 paper on automated prompt optimization using LLM-powered Genetic Algorithms. IIT Kharagpur + ISB.
contacts.subject: RAG + agentic systems -- worth a quick chat?
contacts.content:
Hi Anant,

Came across the Gen Academy work you and Arvind put together -- training 2,000 engineers on RAG, MCP, and context engineering in six months is a real signal about where the market is moving. The context engineering workshop in particular stood out; I've been working through exactly that problem in production, figuring out how much context is too much before retrieval quality starts hurting response accuracy.

Quick context on my end: at Vivansh I built a 2-stage LangGraph orchestration system where the first stage handles 250+ permission checks per query before any retrieval happens, and the second stage hits a Milvus index with 10,000+ chunks from 750+ courses. Response time holds at ~8s under 500+ concurrent users. Before that at Silver Touch, I ran a head-to-head benchmark of Milvus, Qdrant, and FAISS on 50k+ monthly production queries -- the 95% vs 45% MRR gap between Milvus and FAISS is what convinced us to migrate.

If TrueFoundry has room on the ML side, I'd love 15 minutes to hear how you're thinking about the applied GenAI problems your team is working through. Tuesday or Thursday next week works for me.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: RAG + agentic systems -- worth a quick chat?
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Anant,

Following up in case this got lost. I read your IEEE paper on LLM-powered genetic algorithms for prompt optimization after sending the last note -- the fitness function design for measuring prompt quality on open-ended generation is a cleaner approach than the manual iteration I've been doing in production.

If the timing isn't right, no worries.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Joined TrueFoundry Sept 2024; builds and deploys AI chat agents; integrates LLM eval frameworks into the platform. Former Lead Data Scientist at Delhivery. Her work wiring LLM evaluation into a production platform is the same class of problem Amit faced benchmarking 6 embedding models on production data before selecting BGE-M3 + reranker.
contacts.subject: LLM eval frameworks in production -- quick question
contacts.content:
Hi Aditi,

Was reading about the LLM eval work you've been wiring into TrueFoundry's platform. That problem of building evaluation that actually reflects production behavior -- not just benchmark scores -- is one I've been dealing with directly.

At Silver Touch I ran a head-to-head on 6 embedding models (BGE small/base/large, Stella, SPLADE, BGE-M3) using our live query traffic rather than any standard benchmark. MRR on production data was the only honest signal. BGE-M3 plus a reranker won, and the entire migration from FAISS to Milvus improved MRR from 45% to 95%. At Vivansh the same evaluation-first mindset shaped how I built a 2-stage LangGraph retrieval system: measure permission-filtering precision, measure retrieval recall, measure end-to-end latency under 500+ concurrent users. Iterating without those numbers is guessing.

If your team has room for someone who approaches AI engineering this way, I'd be glad to hear what you're building. Worth a 15-min call?

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: LLM eval frameworks in production -- quick question
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Aditi,

Bumping this up. Since I sent the last email, I've been thinking more about the gap between offline eval and production behavior for chat agents specifically -- the permission-aware RAG system I run has cases where offline MRR looks fine but live queries surface edge cases that only show up with real user permission profiles.

No pressure if the timing isn't right.

Amit

contacts.subject: ML platform engineering -- your team hiring?
contacts.content:
Hi Mitanshu,

Came across your profile while going through TrueFoundry's team -- the ML + backend combination is exactly the kind of work I've been doing in production.

At Vivansh I built a LangGraph-orchestrated RAG system from scratch: 2-stage Milvus retrieval, 250+ live permission checks per query, 500+ concurrent users. The part that took the most engineering was the abstraction layer between the LangGraph graph logic and the underlying FastAPI + PostgreSQL data layer -- getting that boundary clean so we could iterate on the retrieval strategy without touching the agent logic. At Silver Touch before that, I ran the ML infra rebuild: FAISS to Milvus migration, in-code Llama-2-7b to VLLM-served API, 50k+ queries/month in production. That project is why I care about how ML platforms are actually structured.

Worth connecting if TrueFoundry is growing the platform engineering team? I'd talk to whoever makes sense on your side.

Amit

No pressure if you're not the right person to connect with on this.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Won accelData Hackathon top spot with team-not-found for an AWS resource management project. Passionate about AI and homelabs. His hackathon AWS work parallels Amit's serverless AWS Lambda pipeline for video course rendering at Vivansh.
contacts.subject: fellow ML engineer -- TrueFoundry team expanding?
contacts.content:
Hi Kunwar,

Saw you won the accelData hackathon top spot for the AWS resource management project -- nice work. The AWS angle caught my attention because I recently shipped a serverless video rendering pipeline on Lambda for a course generation system at Vivansh: FFmpeg rendering jobs offloaded from EC2, SAM CLI for deployment, S3 for intermediate and final assets. The constraint was keeping the main server free while processing 10,000+ video clips.

Broadly I'm 2 years into production ML: LangGraph-based agentic RAG at Vivansh (250+ permissions enforced per query, 500+ concurrent users, 80% MRR), and before that a full infra rebuild at Silver Touch (FAISS to Milvus, in-code LLM to VLLM-served API, 50k+ queries/month). That's the kind of system I want to keep building.

Is TrueFoundry growing the ML engineering team? Worth a quick chat or a pointer to whoever I should talk to.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Has a personal site (kashishkumar.com) and Medium blog documenting his ML work; former ExxonMobil. His public writing habit signals he values sharing technical depth -- same way Amit's benchmark data (FAISS 45% vs Milvus 95% MRR) tells the story better than any credential.
contacts.subject: ML engineer with production RAG + LangGraph -- worth connecting?
contacts.content:
Hi Kashish,

Found your personal site while going through TrueFoundry's team -- the Medium writing habit is a good signal, it usually means someone is actually processing what they're building rather than just shipping and moving on.

I've been doing the same kind of documentation in my own work. The most useful piece I put together was a breakdown of a production vector DB evaluation: FAISS at 45% MRR, Qdrant at 85%, Milvus at 95%, all on 50k+ live queries/month at Silver Touch. That benchmark drove the Milvus migration. At Vivansh after that, I built a LangGraph agentic RAG system that enforces 250+ user permissions per query against a 10,000+ chunk Milvus index. Both are in production; neither is a demo.

If TrueFoundry has room for that kind of engineer, you'd be the right person to connect me with whoever is hiring on the ML side. Worth a quick chat?

Amit

contacts.subject: AI/ML engineer -- production RAG, LangGraph, Milvus
contacts.content:
Hi Parth,

Reaching out directly because TrueFoundry's ML platform stack matches what I've been building in production.

Short version: 2+ years shipping production AI systems. At Vivansh I built a LangGraph-orchestrated agentic RAG chatbot -- 2-stage Milvus retrieval, 250+ live permission checks per query, serving 500+ concurrent users at 80% MRR. Before that at Silver Touch, I rebuilt the RAG infrastructure for a 50k+ query/month platform: migrated from FAISS to Milvus (45% to 95% MRR), moved in-code Llama-2-7b to a VLLM-served API, cut latency from 16s to 8s. Also shipped an AI form builder (GPT-4o + Qwen3-Coder + GPT-4o-mini pipeline, 200+ forms generated in month 1) and a video course generation pipeline (10,000+ source clips, AWS Lambda rendering, 1 week to 10 minutes).

Azure AI Engineer Associate certified. Full profile and projects: amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

If there's an open ML or AI platform role I should be looking at, I'd appreciate a pointer. If not, happy to be on your radar.

Thanks,
Amit
